behavior_summary: This script implements a minimal 'U8P_FTABLEEW::look' (inspect) handler for an object. When the player examines the object, the process emits a short bark reading 'table ' and then ends.
No menus, branching, flag reads/writes, or external calls are present — it serves purely as an inspection/description response.
notable_control_flow: Single linear execution path: the U8P_FTABLEEW::look process runs, produces one Bark ('table '), and then the process suspends/ends.
There are two low-confidence Unknown nodes in the decompiled flow (likely framing/suspend nodes) but they do not introduce conditional logic or additional behavior.
open_questions: Why the bark text contains a trailing space ('table '); is this intentional (concatenation with other text elsewhere) or an artifact of extraction?
Unknown nodes in the decompiled data appear in the function — they do not change observable behavior here, but their original intent is unclear (formatting/suspend wrappers).

main_function.name: U8P_FTABLEEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player inspects or 'looks at' the object by emitting a single bark 'table '.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_FTABLEEW::look/inspect process for this object (process type 0x0208).
Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_FTABLEEW::look' action on this item/entity.
